title: Laboon’s Name and Luffy’s Challenge
description: On pages 8–10, laboon’s name and luffy’s challenge as crocus explains Laboon’s long vigil and the tragedy behind the whale’s attacks on the Red Line.
scene: Laboon’s Name and Luffy’s Challenge. Crocus explains Laboon’s long vigil and the tragedy behind the whale’s attacks on the Red Line. The scene focuses on the immediate character conflict rather than background exposition.

dialogue.speaker: Crocus
dialogue.text: Laboon.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Listen to me, Laboon!

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: They've already..

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: ...Left this place!

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: Laboon!

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: Please listen to me.

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: LA- BO- ON!!!

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: you...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Since that day..

dialogue.speaker: Crocus
dialogue.text: ...Laboon always faces the loose mountain and cries.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: and he has hit his forehead to destroy the red line...

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: ...as a sign that they will come back here.

dialogue.speaker: Crocus
dialogue.text: I've been talking with Laboon many times,

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: But Laboon still doesn't admit

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: The truth.

dialogue.speaker: Crocus
dialogue.text: What a whale! Even though he knows that he was betrayed, he's still waiting.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #5
dialogue.text: Even though it's worthless to wait.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #5
dialogue.text: Yes. Because he will have nothing left if

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: He let down the promise, that's why

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: Why he doesn't want to listen to me. He's afraid of losing the meaning of waiting.

dialogue.speaker: Crocus
dialogue.text: Laboon's hometown is in West Blue

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: On the other side of red line.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #6
dialogue.text: That means he can't go back there anymore.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #6
dialogue.text: So, those people are his only hope and his everything.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: He is surely poor, but you were betrayed, too.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Why do you still

dialogue.speaker: Crocus
dialogue.text: Get involved with him?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #6
dialogue.text: Look at those scars on his forehead.

summary: This part advances the chapter by focusing on laboon’s name and luffy’s challenge. It keeps the main tension clear while preserving the dialogue that drives the scene.
